notes: Palo Alto Networks is a cybersecurity company known for next-generation firewalls and the Cortex security platform. They pioneered application-aware firewalls. Key products: PAN-OS (firewall operating system), GlobalProtect (VPN/endpoint protection), Cortex XDR (extended detection and response), Prisma (cloud security). Unit 42 is their threat intelligence team publishing detailed threat research and IOCs. Like Fortinet, firewall vulnerabilities are high-value targets for nation-state actors. Palo Alto is a CNA. GlobalProtect VPN vulnerabilities are high-risk because they are internet-exposed. Notable: CVE-2024-3400 was a critical PAN-OS command injection actively exploited in the wild; CVE-2024-0012 was an authentication bypass in the management interface. Both appeared in CISA KEV with evidence of nation-state exploitation.

data.description: Palo Alto Networks security advisories covering PAN-OS, GlobalProtect, Cortex, Prisma, and other Palo Alto products.
data.notes: Palo Alto indexes advisories by CVE ID rather than a proprietary ID format. The security portal allows direct lookup by CVE number. Advisory URLs use the CVE ID in the path: https://security.paloaltonetworks.com/CVE-YYYY-NNNN. Covers PAN-OS, GlobalProtect, Cortex, Prisma, and other products. High-profile vulnerabilities because firewalls are critical infrastructure. Palo Alto also publishes PAN-SA-* identifiers in some contexts.

children.description: CVE ID used as Palo Alto advisory identifier

examples.note: Critical PAN-OS command injection, actively exploited
